name: Mama Crow's Raisin-Nut Jam Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300°F.
2. Grease and flour three 9-inch cake pans.
3. In a large bowl, beat butter and sugar until light and fluffy using an electric mixer.
4. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
5. Stir in the jam.
6. In a separate bowl, sift together flour, allspice, cinnamon, baking powder, and baking soda.
7. Alternately add dry ingredients and buttermilk to the butter mixture, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Mix until just combined.
8. Fold in raisins and pecan nuts.
9. Divide batter evenly among prepared cake pans.
10. Bake for 40 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Cool completely on racks.
Frosting:
1. In a heavy saucepan, combine sugar and cream. Heat over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, until it boils.
2. Cook for 2 to 3 minutes, then remove from heat and allow to cool slightly.
3. Beat in butter until the mixture begins to thicken.
4. Spread frosting between layers and on top of the cooled cake. Allow excess to drip down the sides of the cake.
5. Decorate with mixed candied fruits and pecan halves, if desired.
6. Serve with Old-Time Boiled Custard (see separate recipe).
